description_long: This object is a vertical spear-like pole with a pointed metallic tip at the top and a horizontal crossbar supporting a rectangular pennant. The pennant is divided diagonally into two color blocks: a light gray upper left triangle and a deep blue lower right triangle. The pole appears to be made of wood or a wood-like material, with metallic fittings at the tip and possibly at the crossbar. The pennant is attached to the crossbar, which is fixed perpendicular to the main shaft, giving it a flag-like appearance. The overall design is simple and functional, suitable for marking locations or serving as a decorative or signaling element in an urban environment. The object is slender and tall, with the pennant hanging flat and taut.
description: A tall, spear-tipped pole with a crossbar holding a diagonally split blue and gray pennant. The design is simple, with a wooden shaft and metallic fittings.
description_view_0: A side view showing the thin profile of the pole and pennant, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_1: A front view displaying the full pennant with its diagonal blue and gray color split, the spear tip, and the crossbar.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to view 0, showing the narrow edge of the pole and pennant.
description_view_3: A rear view of the pennant, mirroring the front with the same color split and visible spear tip and crossbar.
attribute_graphic_design: The pennant features a diagonal split with the upper left half in light gray and the lower right half in deep blue. There are no visible logos, text, or symbols.
